original: Battle at Monte Levunium: Byzantium Emperor Alexius I beats Petshegene force invading from the north
Emperor Charles declares all privileges of Ghent ended
EmperorCharles Vgrants inquisitors additional powers
A Flemish woman introduces the practice of starching linen into England
Merchant traveller Ralph Fitch returns to England after a journey of eight years, including the first extensive exploration of India and South East Asia by an English person
11 Dutch ships depart for the conquest of Peru
Mughal Emperor Shan Jahan founds his new capital city Shahjahanabad (modern Old Delhi)
Farm leader Li Zicheng becomes Emperor of China and flees Peking
Beginning of 18 day battle of Zhovti Vody in Ukraine - Polish King John II Casimir eventually defeated by Cossacks
Chinese Ming dynasty occupies Taiwan
Clemens X [Emilio Bonaventura Altieri] elected Pope
Emperor Jozef I becomes monarch of Cologne and Bavaria
English and Scottish parliaments accept Act of Union; creates the United Kingdom of Great Britain (comes into being 1st May)
English AstronomerJohn Flamsteedobserves Uranus for 6th time
The New York Gazette and Post Boy carries first public report of a cricket match played in America; New York XI v London XI on the site of what is today the Fulton Fish Market in Manhattan, NYC
French fleet occupies Tobago during American War of Independence
French fleet stops the Britain from seizing the Cape of Good Hope
Cornerstone laid for Groningen's new town hall in the Netherlands
First US rubber patent is granted to Jacob F. Hummel
Macon B. Allen and Robert Morris Jr. are the first African Americans to open a law practice in the US
Comet C/1853 G1 (Schweizer) approaches within 0.0839 AUs of Earth
Ashmun Institute (later Lincoln University), in Hinsonville, Pennsylvania, receives its charter from Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, making it the 1st degree-granting Black college in US
US Army, Pacific Div HQ permanently forms at Presidio (San Francisco)
Maryland's House of Delegates votes against seceding from Union (US Civil War)
100,000 federal troops prepare to march into Corinth, Mississippi
Battle of Grand Gulf, Mississippi: Union navy ironclad warships attack Confederate fortifications
-30] Skirmish at Jenkins' Ferry, Arkansas
The Theta Xi fraternity is founded at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute in Troy, New York
1st public Dutch electricity service begins
Charlie Reilly becomes one of baseball's first pinch hitters
The 500-strong Commonwealth of Christ (Coxey's Army) arrives in Washington, D.C., to protest against unemployment; Coxey arrested for trespassing at Capitol
Antisemitic riot in Budapest
Congress extends the Chinese Exclusion Act (of 1882) prohibiting immigration of Chinese laborers from territories to the mainland, a rule clearly aimed at Chinese in the Philippines
Limestone slide at Turtle Mountain drops 30 million cubic metres (82 million tons) on to town of Frank, Alberta, Canada, kills 70-90 residents
2" rain falls in 10 mins in Taylor, Texas
108°F (42°C), Tuguegarao, Philippines (Oceania record)
Duke Kahanamoku wins his 2nd gold medal of the day in the winning American 4 x 200m freestyle relay team with Perry McGillivray, Pua Kealoha and Norman Ross in world record 10:04.4
First official International Weightlifting Federation Championship is held in Tallinn, Estonia
France and US reach accord on repayment of WW I
Construction of Spirit of St Louis (the monoplane which Charles Lindburgh was to fly across the Atlantic) is completed
123 runs are scored in 7 major league games
North Sea floodgate at Ijmuiden (biggest in world) officially opens
Telephone connection Britain-Australia goes into service
First broadcast of "One Man's Family" on NBC radio, the longest-running dramatic serial on US radio (ends 1959)
Pittsburgh is last major league baseball city to play a home game on a Sunday, as the Pirates host the Cincinnati Reds
1st pro baseball game in Japan is played Nagoya defeats Daitokyo, 8-5
Symbolic 'Golden Rivet', completing the Golden Gate Bridge connecting San Francisco and Marin County, is driven (later replaced with a more structurally sound steel version)
Whitestone Bridge connecting the New York boroughs of Bronx and Queens opens
1st radio broadcast of "Young Dr Malone" on CBS
Norwegian King Haakon & government flee to Britain
Robert Sherwood's play "There Shall be No Night" premieres in NYC
Japanese troop march into Lashio, cutting off Burma Road
Jews forced to wear a Jewish Star in Netherlands & Vichy-France
German pastor and anti-Nazi dissident Dietrich Bonhoeffer arrested by the Gestapo
Milk Strike: Strikes against forced labor during the Second World War begin in the Netherlands, the largest strike in Dutch history
US 34th Division occupies Hill 609, North Tunisia
Raid by Dutch Resistance on the National Printing Office in The Hague to procure 10,000 Dutch identity cards.
First food drop by RAF above Nazi-occupied Holland (Operation Manna)
German armies in Italy sign an unconditional surrender to the Western Allies to be carried out on 2 May
Japanese army evacuates Rangoon, British Burma
The General Labour Federation of Belgium forms
US Army liberates 31,601 people from the Dachau Nazi concentration camp in Germany
Venice and Mestre captured by the Allies
28 former Japanese leaders indicted in Tokyo as war criminals

China seizes the assets of the Asiatic Petroleum Company, a joint venture between the Shell and Royal Dutch oil companies in retaliation for the Hong Kong Government's requisitioning of the tanker Yung Hao
6th Cannes Film Festival: "The Wages of Fear" directed by Henri-Georges Clouzot wins the Grand Prix du Festival International du Film
First experimental 3D-TV broadcast shows an episode of Space Patrol on Los Angeles ABC affiliate KECA-TV
Milwaukee Braves' Joe Adcock is 1st to homer into New York's Polo Grounds center field bleachers
Giovanni Gronchi elected third President of Italy
WLUC TV channel 6 in Marquette, MI (CBS/NBC/ABC) begins broadcasting
WSPA TV channel 7 in G'ville-Spartanburg, SC (CBS) begins broadcasting
WWBT TV channel 12 in Richmond, VA (NBC) begins broadcasting
1st military nuclear power plant dedicated at Fort Belvoir, Virginia
ABC's "Wide World of Sports" debuts
16th Tony Awards: "A Man For All Seasons" (play) & "How to Succeed in Business Without Really Trying" (musical) win
KRE-AM in Berkeley CA changes call letters to KPAT
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Australian government announces it would send troops to Vietnam
Earthquake hits Seattle; 5 die
Malta becomes 18th member of Council of Europe
Galt MacDermot, Gerome Ragni and James Rado's hippie musical "Hair" opens  at Biltmore Theater, NYC; runs for 1750 performances
US and South Vietnamese forces launch an incursion into Cambodia, expanding the Vietnam War
Boeing receives contract for Mariner 10, Mercury exploration
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Charles McMahon and Darwin Judge are the last two United States servicemen killed in Vietnam during the Vietnam War
Ethiopia nationalizes all land
Minister Irene Vorrink begins fluoridating Dutch drinking water
British Aerospace forms
Corazones Unidos Siempre Chi Upsilon Sigma National Latin Sorority Inc. is founded
NFL Draft: Oklahoma running back Billy Sims first pick by Detroit Lions
NFL Draft: South Carolina running back George Rogers first pick by New Orleans Saints
Alfredo Magana elected President of El Salvador
17th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (51-B): Challenger 7 launches
Ranger Larry Parrish is 5th to hit 3 HRs in a game in both leagues
Tony Tubbs beats defending champion Greg Page by unanimous decision in 15 rounds at Memorial Auditorium, Buffalo for WBA heavyweight boxing title
800,000 books destroyed by fire in Los Angeles Central Library
Japan's premier Nakasone visits the US
MLB Baltimore Orioles end their record 21-game losing streak by beating the Chicago White Sox 9-0
Second Lubbers government falls in the Netherlands
US 66th manned space mission STS 31 (Discovery 10) returns from space
Wrecking cranes begin tearing down the Berlin Wall at the Brandenburg Gate
Cyclone strikes the Chittagong district in Bangladesh, killing 139,000 people and leaving 10 million homeless
Earthquake in Georgia, kills 100
American country singer Doug Stone, 35, undergoes quadruple bypass surgery
Sheena Easton collapses on stage while performing in "Man of LaMancha"
Ferry boat smashes into Mombasa Harbor Kenya, kills over 300
Israel and PLO sign an economic accord
Last day of voting in 1st multi-racial elections in South Africa
Final TV broadcast of "Empty Nest" on NBC TV
KC Royal John Nonely is 70th to HR on his 1st at bat
Longest sausage ever, at 28.77 miles, made in Kitchener, Ontario
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in Fresno, California on KFRR 104.1 FM
Chemical Weapons Convention of 1993 comes into force, outlawing the production, stockpiling, and use of chemical weapons among its signatories
MLB Kansas City Royals Chili Davis is 75th to hit 300 HRs
Avala TV Tower near Belgrade is destroyed in NATO bombing of Yugoslavia
The United States is re-elected to the United Nations Commission on Human Rights, one year after losing the seat it had held for 50 years
Oldsmobile builds its final car ending 107 years of production
New Zealand's first civil union takes place
Syria completes withdrawal from Lebanon, ending 29 years of occupation
NFL Draft: North Carolina State defensive end Mario Williams first pick from Houston Texans
Second Republic protest in support of state secularism is held in Istanbul, Turkey
International Chemical Weapons Convention deadline for chemical weapon stockpiles comes into effect
7 people are killed after a Boeing 747 crashes in Bagram, Afghanistan
German Measles is declared eradicated from North and South America - 1st world region to do so
Indonesia executes 8 prisoners, including 7 foreigners, for drug offences at Besi Prison on the island of Nusakambangan
"H.O.L.Y" single released by Florida Georgia Line (Billboard Song of the Year, 2016)
Iranian run-off elections give moderates and reforms a majority in parliament
Three tornadoes hit southeast of Dallas, Texas, killing five
Sweden's official Twitter account confirms Swedish meatballs actually originated in Turkey
UK Home Secretary Amber Rudd resigns amid immigration scandal involving the Windrush generation
Indonesia announces plan to relocate its capital from Jakarta due to the city sinking, according to a government minister
Islamic State releases video showing leader Bakr al-Baghdadi for first time in five years
Over 300 people declared to have died through overwork in Indonesia's one-day election on April 17th, with over 2,000 fallen sick
Over 700 people infected with measles in the US, highest number for 25 years
Sports Illustrated features a Muslim model, Halima Aden, in a Burkini for the first time in their swimsuit edition
A mile-wide asteroid named 1998 OR2 passes within 3.9 million miles of Earth
Fire at an Icheon warehouse, South Korea, kills 38
UK official death toll reaches 26,000 deaths from COVID-19 as care home and community deaths included
US GDP falls 4.8% for the financial quarter, officially ending the country's growth streak. Worst contraction since 2008.
World record for the longest single lightning flash of 477miles (768km) across US states of Texas, Louisiana and Mississippi according to World Meteorological Organization
Brazil's official COVID-19 death toll passes 400,000, with daily fatalities at 3,000, down from 4,000
World's longest pedestrian bridge at 516 metres (1,700 feet) opens inside northern Portugal's Arouca Geopark
Six-story building collapses in the Chinese city of Changsha, killing 26, with one woman surviving six days in the rubble
72-hour ceasefire in Sudan breaks down between the Sudanese army and paramilitary Rapid Support Forces, as air and artillery strikes on Khartoum resume
Scotland’s first minister, Humza Yousaf resigns after his coalition government fails
Minnesota Wild Marc-Andre Fleury (40) appears in his record 18th Stanley Cup playoff, most for any NHL goaltender
Severe storms in Western Pennsylvania leave 400,000 without power and three people dead
Joan of Arcarrives at the siege of Orleans
Holy Roman EmperorCharles Vnames Frans Van der Hulst inquisitor-general of the Seventeen Provinces in the Netherlands
English naval officerFrancis Drakesails into Cadiz, Spain and sinks the Spanish fleet, thereby "Singeing the King of Spain's Beard" and delaying the Spanish invasion by a year
Sweden and Denmark sign a defense treaty against the Catholic DukeAlbrecht von Wallenstein
PrinceFrederick Henryoccupies Schenkenschans fortress after a nine-month siege during the Dutch Revolt
Franco-Dutch War:Louis XIVof France invades the Netherlands
Scottish engineerJames Watt's patent for a steam engine with a separate condenser enrolled (Patent 913)
Premiere ofMozart's "Violin Sonata No. 32 "in B flat (K. 454) at Kärntnerthor Theater in Vienna; violinist Regina Strinasacchi, and Wolfgang Mozart on piano
Charles Darwin's expedition sees the top of the Andes Mountains from Patagonia
First edition of PeterRoget's Thesaurus is published in Britain and has never been out of print since
Jesse James' gang robs a bank of $1,500 in Columbia, Kentucky, killing bank employee Robert A.C. Martin during the crime
The "Elektromote" - forerunner of the trolleybus - is tested byWerner von Siemensin Berlin
Old Kavallison, Congo:Henry Morton Stanleymeets Emin Pasha
Pierre Savorgnan de Brazzalands in Libreville, Gabon
Ex-US PresidentTheodore Rooseveltvisits Amsterdam
Frank Wedekind's play "Tod und Teufel" premieres in Berlin
Tris Speakerties career outfield record of 4 unassisted double plays
Cleveland Indians pitcherWes Ferrellno-hits St Louis Browns, 9-0 at League Park II, Cleveland
Noël Coward's comic play "Present Laughter" premieres in London
Conscientious objectorDesmond Dosssaves 75 wounded soldiers in the Battle of Okinawa at Hacksaw Ridge. Later depicted in the Oscar-winning film "Hacksaw Ridge".
Canadian folk singer-songwriter Joni Anderson (soon to be known asJoni Mitchell) meets American folk singer and future husband Chuck Mitchell at a gig in Toronto, Ontario
Aretha Franklinreleases her single "Respect" (written byOtis Redding), Billboard Song of the Year 1967
US PresidentRichard Nixonsaid he will release edited tapes made in the White House
MLB Philadelphia PhilliesSteve Carltonis 1st lefty to strike out 3,000 batters
American mafia hitmanRichard Kuklinskimurders pharmacist Paul Hoffman by beating him with a tire iron
Harold Washingtonsworn in as Chicago's 1st African American mayor
New York Yankees fire managerYogi Berra16 games into season, despite assurance from ownerGeorge Steinbrennerthat he would be kept for the whole season.Billy Martinnamed as replacement.
Boston Red Sox pitcherRoger Clemensstrikes out 20 Seattle Mariners
NFL Draft: Auburn running backBo Jacksonfirst pick by Tampa Bay Buccaneers
Chicago CubAndre Dawsonhits for cycle
Dan Quisenberry(all-time AL save king, 238) announces his retirement
"Don't Rock the Jukebox" single released byAlan Jackson(ASCAP Award Country Song of the Year, 1992; Billboard Song of the Year, 1991)
Voting ends on choice ofElvis Presleypostage stamp
Canadian musicianDrakereleases "Views", his 4th studio album
Animated series "The Simpsons" surpasses 635-episode count of "Gunsmoke"'; highest number of episodes of any series on TV
Spanish tennis greatRafael Nadalequals his Open-era record for most wins at the same event (11 Monte Carlo) with his 11th Barcelona ATP title

US PresidentJoe Bidenand First Lady JillBidenvisit former US PresidentJimmy Carterand former First Lady Rosalynn Carter at the Carters’ home in Plains, Georgia; Carter (96) did not attend the Biden's inauguration due to health concerns

Country singerWillie Nelsonhosts first of two-night 90th birthday concerts for himself at the Hollywood Bowl in Los Angeles, California; guest performers includeLyle Lovett;Neil Young,Snoop Dogg, George Strait;Rosanne Cash,Kris Kristofferson,Norah Jones,Chris Stapleton, Beck, Edie Brickell,Tom Jones, and Ziggy Marley, among others
Drenthe, Netherlands, adopts Gregorian calendar, tomorrow is May 12, 1701
Battle of Gate Pā (Pukehinahina): 1,700 British troops suffer their worst defeat of the New Zealand Wars at the hands of 230 entrenched Māori warriors in Tauranga
Irish republicans abandon the post office in Dublin and surrender unconditionally, ending the Easter Rising
US begins to evacuate its citizens from Saigon in Operation Frequent Wind in response to advancing North Vietnamese forces, bringing an end to US involvement in the Vietnam War
Jury acquits Los Angeles Police Department officers on charges of excessive force in the beating ofRodney King; the decision sparks massive riots in the city
Dick Cheneyand George W. Bush testify before the 9/11 Commission in a closed, unrecorded hearing in the Oval Office
London court sentences retired tennis starBoris Beckerto 2-1/2 years in prison for hiding assets during bankruptcy proceedings
